# Overview

ansicon is a Python wrapper for loading [Jason Hood]'s [ANSICON]

# Installation

```console
$ pip install ansicon
```

# Usage

```python
import ansicon

# Load ansicon
ansicon.load()

# Have fun with terminal codes
print(u'\x1b[32mGreen\x1b[m')

# Unload ansicon
ansicon.unload()

# Check if ansicon is loaded
if not ansicon.loaded():
    ansicon.load()
if ansicon.loaded():
    ansicon.unload()
```

[ansicon]: https://github.com/adoxa/ansicon
[jason hood]: https://github.com/adoxa

**ANSICON Python module**
A Python wrapper for loading Jason Hood's ANSICON

functions.name: ansicon._get_dll_path
documentation: Determines the path of the ANSICON DLL

classes.name: ansicon._Loader

methods.name: ansicon._Loader.load
documentation:
Load the DLL
Does nothing if the DLL is already loaded.
Will raise an exception, most likely WindowsError, if it fails.

methods.name: ansicon._Loader.unload
documentation:
Unload the DLL
Does nothing if the DLL has not been loaded.
Will raise a WindowsError exception if it fails.

methods.name: ansicon._Loader.loaded
documentation: Returns True if the the DLL was previously loaded

DLL Loader
Keeps track of DLL handle for unloading
